words.meaning: To perform or complete a task
words.exampleSentence: The team will carry out the experiment tomorrow.

words.meaning: To find or meet by chance
words.exampleSentence: I came across an old photograph while cleaning the attic.

words.meaning: To stop functioning or to analyze in detail
words.exampleSentence: Let me break down the problem into smaller parts.

words.meaning: To mention a topic or raise a child
words.exampleSentence: She brought up an important point during the meeting.
They had to call off the event due to bad weather.

words.meaning: To think of or produce an idea
words.exampleSentence: We need to come up with a creative solution.

words.meaning: To reduce the amount of something
words.exampleSentence: The doctor advised me to cut down on sugar.

words.meaning: To handle or manage a situation
words.exampleSentence: We must deal with this issue immediately.

words.meaning: To finally be in a particular situation
words.exampleSentence: If you keep spending like this, you'll end up in debt.

words.meaning: To have a good relationship with someone
words.exampleSentence: She gets along with all her colleagues.

We went through a difficult period last year.

words.meaning: To restrain or prevent from advancing
words.exampleSentence: She held back her tears during the speech.

words.meaning: To stay at the same level or pace

words.exampleSentence: It's hard to keep up with the latest technology.

words.meaning: To take care of someone or something
words.exampleSentence: Can you look after my cat while I'm away?

Her talent makes her stand out from the crowd.
She takes after her mother in both looks and personality.

words.meaning: To reject or refuse an offer
words.exampleSentence: She turned down the job offer because of the low salary.

words.meaning: To be careful or alert about
words.exampleSentence: Watch out for hidden costs in the contract.

words.meaning: To enter forcibly or start suddenly

words.meaning: To do something that has been delayed
words.exampleSentence: I need to catch up on my reading this weekend.

words.meaning: To leave school or a course before completion

words.meaning: To lag behind others in progress

words.meaning: To recover from an illness or shock

Hold on to your dreams no matter what.

I look forward to meeting you next week.

words.meaning: To understand or see with difficulty

words.meaning: To make a mistake or spoil something
words.exampleSentence: I messed up the presentation and felt embarrassed.

words.meaning: To reduce the number of options

words.meaning: To succeed in doing something difficult

words.meaning: To propose or suggest an idea
words.exampleSentence: She put forward a brilliant proposal.

words.meaning: To depend on someone or something
words.exampleSentence: You can always rely on her for honest advice.

words.meaning: To eliminate or exclude a possibility

words.meaning: To start living a stable life

words.meaning: To express opinions boldly or louder
words.exampleSentence: If you disagree, you should speak up.

words.meaning: To leave the ground or become successful
words.exampleSentence: His career really took off after the award.

You should try on the dress before buying it.
We used up all the paint on this project.

words.exampleSentence: Everything will work out in the end.

words.meaning: To support or make a copy
words.exampleSentence: You should back up your files regularly.

words.meaning: To reveal feelings or become available

words.meaning: To meet by chance or encounter

words.meaning: To start a journey or cause explosion

words.meaning: To discard or get rid of

words.meaning: To explain or be the reason for

words.meaning: To refresh one's knowledge or skill

words.exampleSentence: She pored over the documents all night.
